receipt: MEASURED tick 241 (was NEVER MEASURED): grep for content-security-policy across engine/*/src returns ZERO hits — CSP is unimplemented BY CONSTRUCTION, not merely untested, which is stronger than a failed fixture because that cannot distinguish a missing feature from a bad case. receipt: tick 266: scroll-snap-type/scroll-snap-align parse (MinimalCascade, recovered into the Stylo path like text-overflow/overflow-wrap), round-trip through getComputedStyle (camelCase AND getPropertyValue), and a scroll LANDS on the nearest snap point. Implemented as ONE transformation at the single scroll chokepoint (Page::set_element_scroll), snapping AFTER the clamp so the last slide is reachable. Candidates are collected from the CONTAINER's own subtree, so one carousel cannot snap to another's slide. mandatory/proximity strictness deliberately NOT modelled (both conform to 'nearest'). RESIDUE: the VERTICAL axis is gated and works; the HORIZONTAL axis could not be gated because an inline-block row yields NO horizontal scroll range in layout today (max_x = 0) — a pre-existing scroll-geometry gap, NOT a snap gap, and it means real horizontal carousels do not yet scroll at all, let alone snap.

receipt: tick 242 WIRED the dead-end: html5ever's verdict now rides on Dom::quirks (a field, so NO signature changes anywhere — every consumer already receives a &Dom, incl. all 18 cascade_styles call sites). Reaches Stylo via cascade_via_stylo + StyloDocument::quirks_mode, and JS via document.compatMode. Stylo already IMPLEMENTS the quirks (unitless lengths, case-insensitive id/class, <font size> table) — it was never told the mode. TWO parse paths both needed it: StyloStylesheet::from_str (<style>/linked) AND parse_style_attribute (inline style=), and legacy markup — exactly what lands in quirks — is overwhelmingly inline-styled. Gate asserts RENDERING and REPORTING agree in both directions plus that the modes actually differ (WPT quirks/unitless-length: width:100 => 100px in quirks, dropped in standards). LimitedQuirks folds to false (it does not enable the unitless quirk). Full page suite re-run: 122 passed / 1 pre-existing hard_wall failure, no regressions from doctype-less fixtures becoming quirks. tick 243 COMPLETED it: all remaining MatchingContext/media-query sites now read qm_of(el.dom), AND — the trap — our own RuleIndex by_id/by_class buckets are keyed through the same index_key(v,qm), because telling the matcher 'case-insensitive' while the index keys by exact case discards the rule BEFORE matching (proven: reverting index_key alone makes #FOO give 800px not 250px). So `#FOO` matches id=foo in quirks and not in standards. ⚠ GATE NOT IN THE VERIFY WALL — see docs/loop/GATE-COVERAGE.md

what_breaks_without_it: GitHub 1k-5k-line blobs & long PR threads stutter
receipt: MEASURED tick 241 (was 'no node budget'): the verify wall's F-floors already exercise a single large DOM — the 'large' fixture is 357.2KB / 8808 nodes, and a full pipeline runs ~150ms (parse 6.6 / cascade 6.5 / layout 98 / dlist 6.4 / paint 30). So the claim 'F1/F2 don't cover a big single DOM' was itself stale: they do, at ~8.8k nodes, which brackets GitHub's 1k-5k-line blobs. THE SHAPE IS THE FINDING: layout is 65% of the pipeline and is the only super-linear term (mid 1208 nodes -> 13ms; large 8808 nodes -> 98ms, i.e. 7.3x nodes for 7.5x layout, so roughly LINEAR, not quadratic — no accidental O(n^2)). Remaining genuine unknown is not throughput but INTERACTIVITY on such a page (scroll/selection/re-layout after an edit), which F1/F2 do not model
